description_long: This is a large, single-family house with a complex, multi-gabled roof covered in gray shingles. The exterior features a combination of beige horizontal siding and stonework along the lower facade, giving it a Craftsman-inspired appearance. The front of the house includes a covered porch supported by columns, with steps leading up to the main entryway. Multiple rooflines and dormers add architectural interest, while the windows are arranged in an asymmetrical pattern, some with decorative trim. The house sits on a raised foundation, and the rear includes additional covered areas and secondary entrances. The overall design suggests a spacious, upscale suburban home with attention to detail in both materials and layout.
description: A large beige house with a multi-gabled gray shingle roof, stone accents, and a covered front porch with columns.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch, columns, steps, and prominent gabled rooflines with stone and siding details.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended roof, minimal window openings, and the solid, continuous siding along the wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a secondary covered porch, additional gables, and a mix of siding and stonework.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a large gable end, few windows, and the raised foundation clearly visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with columns, steps, and a prominent overhang at the main door
